entries.definition: A decentralized, evidence-forward hiring model: work samples, rubrics, and community-aligned review, contrasted in org docs with purely centralized (Wye-style) proxy hiring.
A structured job description: outcomes, constraints, evaluation weights, accommodation, and a linked WCD. The canonical data lives in p31-delta-hiring for this surface.
Work challenge definition: a bounded, time-limited work sample with deliverables, a rubric whose weights sum to 100%, and good vs anti-pattern hints.
A set of scored dimensions, each with a weight. Reviewers and self-assessment in the app both reference the same dimension ids where possible.
A versioned JSON shape for a candidate’s portable export: consent, artifacts, self-assessment, and free-text to reviewers. Schema: schemas/proof-record.schema.json in this package.
In-org motivation token described in P31’s Delta materials. This app shows weights as UI affordance; it does not mint or settle tokens on chain.
A published table in role-packets data: tokens required, equity %, and governance notes. Legal formation and grants determine real equity.
A community grouping (e.g. frontend, design, operations) used in role metadata for browsability.
1–5 sliders on each rubric dimension in the builder. Not a public score; it aligns your self-review with the same dimensions reviewers will use.
A link (repo, url, other) plus optional commit SHA, label, and notes. The proof record lists multiple artifacts for one submission batch.
Optional git commit id for repository artifacts so reviewers can check out the exact state you intended.
Narrative in each role packet and global policy: async-first, written review by default, and a path to request alternatives. Not medical advice; follow org process.
Download of a p31.proofRecord file. Requires both consent checkboxes. Use for backup and official submission when a channel is published.
Load a p31.proofRecord from file; server validates structure and stores a new local copy to avoid clobbering existing drafts without intent.
Draft proofs persist only in this origin’s browser storage until export. Clearing site data can delete them—back up with exports.
JSON in src/data/ is canonical for this app’s UI. The verify script checks referential integrity (roles→WCD) and rubric weight sums.
Org-side process: calibrate, optionally blind, score against the published rubric. This static app may surface reviewer help but is not a backend ATS.
